demographics.living_situation: resident in a residential aged care facility

contact_nok.relationship: residential aged care nursing staff (present and reporting)

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Every time she finishes her midday meal a huge wave of exhaustion hits her, the room spins, and her legs just give out — she's slumped or collapsed three times this week after lunch and we're terrified she'll crack her head open.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The nursing staff have called after hours because she keeps blacking out and falling within an hour or so of lunch and they can't work out why or how to keep her safe.

symptom_narrative.onset: the falls started about five days ago
symptom_narrative.duration: five days, roughly three episodes so far
symptom_narrative.character: profound lightheadedness with the room spinning and visual blurring or 'seeing stars' when she tries to stand after eating, then her legs give way; she also gets sudden overwhelming sleepiness and slumps in her chair straight after a meal
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the staff say it is severe enough that they now keep her in a wheelchair after meals because she is such a high falls risk
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: whole-body weakness and dizziness, no one area
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the staff have noticed it happens like clockwork within about an hour to an hour and a half of eating a big lunch
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: lying down or staying seated seems to settle it; it eases off before the next meal
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: standing up soon after a large midday meal
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no chest pain, no palpitations, no shortness of breath before the collapses; no blood seen in vomit or bowel motions that staff are aware of
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: no falls like this before this week; usually she walks with a frame
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: normally mobilises with a walker but is currently restricted to a wheelchair after meals because of the fall risk

past_medical_history.how_managed: known diagnosis, followed by her usual doctors
takes a blood pressure tablet each morning

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: a morning blood pressure tablet (staff think it is a fast-acting one)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in a residential aged care facility; nursing staff present at the call and acting as informant; usually mobilises with a walker

communication_style.typical_message_style: facility nurse relaying an incident pattern, worried about injury

objective_data_offered.value: heart rate about 72, staff report it doesn't seem to speed up even when she goes lightheaded after meals
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: facility nurse reading a home/ward monitor; not a clinician-measured or continuously monitored value

objective_data_offered.value: oxygen level reads around 97% on the facility finger monitor
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: facility fingertip oximeter, single spot reading, unverified

objective_data_offered.value: temperature 36.5 on the facility thermometer, no fever
the nurse says her blood pressure readings seem to fall after lunch — roughly 100/60 an hour after eating compared with about 145/85 before the meal
objective_data_offered.timing: before vs approximately 60 minutes after a midday meal
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: facility cuff readings relayed by staff, not a standardised or clinician-supervised postural/postprandial measurement; numbers are approximate and unverified

objective_data_offered.value: on video she looks drowsy and lethargic and is slumped slightly in her chair about an hour after her meal; a mild resting tremor of the hand is visible
staff report the collapses all happen after she eats a large, carbohydrate-heavy lunch and when they try to help her up to walk to the bathroom
